page: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 virtual CPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, which provides 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8i.16xlarge demonstrates strong compute and memory capabilities. It achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as a Passmark CPU score of 82985.85. The DDR5 memory architecture delivers a read-only large-block memory bandwidth of 196.85 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the instance processes medium-model LLM prompts at 298.38 tokens/sec and handles static web serving throughput at 107.39 GB/sec, placing it in the top decile for these workloads.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-intensive applications must rely on network-attached storage over the baseline 30 Gbps network interface. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads that require high-speed DDR5 memory and strong multi-core processing without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. It is highly suited for enterprise applications, web serving, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, delivering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but contains no local storage or dedicated GPUs. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance achieves top-tier benchmark results, including a Passmark CPU score of 82985.85 and a static web serving throughput of 107.39 GB/sec. It also supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX, enabling efficient CPU-based LLM prompt processing. With a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, the m8i.16xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced enterprise workloads, software compilation, database operations, and web serving, though it relies entirely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier CPU performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform featuring sixty-four virtual cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 480 MB L3 cache running at 3.9 GHz
Top-tier CPU performance with a Passmark CPU score of 82985.85
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated vector and matrix math
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for enterprise web serving, databases, and CPU-based inference
